title: Deploy muno to muno.mestumre.dev with Auth0 authentication (post-tutorial Round 8)
description: Two coupled deliverables: (a) wire Auth0 authentication into the muno React app (SPA mode, `@auth0/auth0-react`), replacing the v1 'Guest mode' with real user accounts; (b) deploy the resulting container to a fresh GKE subdomain `muno.mestumre.dev` behind the project's existing Cloudflare-fronted ingress. Frontend-only Auth0 in this round — NoETL server JWT validation is documented as a follow-up. user_uid (from Auth0 `sub` claim) flows into chat events and Firestore paths via the existing event-sourcing pattern. Cap: 1 muno PR (Auth0 + manifests) + 1 ops PR (Helm or kustomize ingress entry for muno.mestumre.dev — verify the existing pattern under repos/ops/ci/manifests/). Pre-handoff items for Kadyapam: Auth0 SPA app registration + Maps key referrer update — see `pre_handoff_for_kadyapam`. Mid-round handoff: Kadyapam adds the DNS record at Cloudflare once the Ingress IP is known (phase 7 surfaces it). Scoping references: `sync/issues/2026-05-12-trip-planner-app-scoping.md` 'muno is the home base' + post-tutorial roadmap item #3.

pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.must_be_done_before_firing: Three items, ~10 minutes total. The round AMBERs at phase 1 if any of these isn't ready.
pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.step_1_auth0_spa_app_registration:
In the existing Auth0 tenant dashboard, create a new Application:
  - Type: **Single Page Application**
  - Name: `Adiona / muno`
  - **Allowed Callback URLs**: `https://muno.mestumre.dev/callback, http://localhost:5173/callback`
  - **Allowed Logout URLs**: `https://muno.mestumre.dev, http://localhost:5173`
  - **Allowed Web Origins**: `https://muno.mestumre.dev, http://localhost:5173`
  - **Allowed Origins (CORS)**: same as Web Origins.
  - Capture the **Domain** (something like `<tenant>.us.auth0.com`) and the **Client ID** (no client secret for SPAs — they use PKCE).
  - Optional but recommended now: create an Auth0 API resource with audience `https://api.muno.mestumre.dev` so future NoETL JWT validation has an identifier ready. SPA app's 'APIs' tab → authorize this API for the SPA.
  - Provision the values into the existing GCP Secret Manager (they're non-secret for SPAs but storing centralizes ops):
    ```bash
    echo -n '<auth0-domain>' | gcloud secrets create muno-auth0-domain \
      --replication-policy=automatic --project=noetl-demo-19700101 --data-file=-
    echo -n '<auth0-client-id>' | gcloud secrets create muno-auth0-client-id \
      --replication-policy=automatic --project=noetl-demo-19700101 --data-file=-
    echo -n 'https://api.muno.mestumre.dev' | gcloud secrets create muno-auth0-audience \
      --replication-policy=automatic --project=noetl-demo-19700101 --data-file=-
    for s in muno-auth0-domain muno-auth0-client-id muno-auth0-audience; do
      gcloud secrets add-iam-policy-binding $s \
        --project=noetl-demo-19700101 \
        --member=serviceAccount:noetl-worker-mcp@noetl-demo-19700101.iam.gserviceaccount.com \
        --role=roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor
    done
    ```
pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.step_2_maps_key_referrer_update:
The `google-maps-widget-key` API key currently has referrer restrictions for `https://mestumre.dev/*`, `https://gateway.mestumre.dev/*`, `https://*.pages.dev/*`. Add the new subdomain:
  - Open https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials?project=noetl-demo-19700101
  - Click `travel-agent-widget-key` → Application restrictions → HTTP referrers
  - Add: `https://muno.mestumre.dev/*`
  - Also (for local dev convenience): `http://localhost:5173/*` if not already there
  - Save.
  - Phase 1 verifies the new referrer was added by `gcloud services api-keys describe` reading.
pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.step_3_confirm_existing_infrastructure:
These are READ-ONLY checks the codex sandbox confirms in phase 1:
  - GKE cluster `noetl-cluster` in us-central1 reachable.
  - The existing ingress controller (probably ingress-nginx) is installed in the cluster.
  - cert-manager is installed and `gateway.mestumre.dev` already has a valid TLS cert — codex confirms `kubectl -n cert-manager get certificate -A | grep mestumre`.
  - GitHub Container Registry push access for ghcr.io/noetl/muno from the codex/operator environment.
  - Cloudflare zone `mestumre.dev` is active (Kadyapam confirms via Cloudflare dashboard; codex doesn't have Cloudflare API access).
pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.mid_round_handoff_dns:
After phase 7 reports the Ingress IP, Kadyapam adds the DNS record at Cloudflare:
  - Cloudflare dashboard → `mestumre.dev` zone → DNS → Records → **Add record**.
  - Type: `A` (or `CNAME` if pointing at the gateway record, but A direct to the Ingress IP is simpler).
  - Name: `muno`.
  - IPv4 address: the Ingress IP from phase 7's output.
  - Proxy status: **Proxied** (orange cloud) — gives us Cloudflare's TLS termination + WAF + caching at the edge.
  - TTL: Auto.
  - SSL/TLS encryption mode for the zone should already be **Full (Strict)** since gateway.mestumre.dev works that way; codex doesn't change this.
  - Save.
  - Wait 1-2 minutes for propagation (often faster).
  - Tell codex 'DNS added' so it can resume to phase 9 (smoke against the live URL).

design_brief_for_codex.auth0_frontend_integration:
Add Auth0 to `muno/src/`:

  - **Deps**: `npm install @auth0/auth0-react`.
  - **Init**: in `muno/src/main.tsx`, wrap `<App />` with `<Auth0Provider domain={import.meta.env.VITE_AUTH0_DOMAIN} clientId={import.meta.env.VITE_AUTH0_CLIENT_ID} authorizationParams={{ redirect_uri: window.location.origin + '/callback', audience: import.meta.env.VITE_AUTH0_AUDIENCE }} cacheLocation="localstorage" useRefreshTokens={true}>`.
  - **Routes**: add `/callback` route that uses `useAuth0().handleRedirectCallback()` then navigates to `/`. React Router config update.
  - **Sidebar**: update `muno/src/components/shell/Sidebar.tsx`:
    - When `isAuthenticated`: show user.name + user.picture (Avatar from MUI Icons or img tag) at the top, replacing 'Guest mode'. A 'Sign out' button below the nav. Triggers `logout({ logoutParams: { returnTo: window.location.origin } })`.
    - When NOT authenticated: show a 'Sign in' button (replaces the prior 'Guest mode' label). Clicking it calls `loginWithRedirect({ authorizationParams: { audience: import.meta.env.VITE_AUTH0_AUDIENCE } })`.
  - **API client**: extend `muno/src/api/noetlClient.ts` to attach the Auth0 access token as a Bearer header when authenticated. Use `useAuth0().getAccessTokenSilently()` to fetch fresh tokens. If unauthenticated, send no token (guest mode preserved as fallback).
  - **user_uid wiring**: when sending events to the itinerary agent (`POST /api/agent/turn` or whatever NoETL endpoint the existing client uses), include `user_uid: user.sub` (Auth0 sub) in the request body if authenticated, else omit (agent falls back to chat_threads/thread_id path).
  - **Locale strings**: add to `muno/src/locales/en.json` and `ru.json`: `sidebar.signin`, `sidebar.signout`, `auth.loading`, `auth.callback_error`.
  - **Vite env vars**: document `VITE_AUTH0_DOMAIN`, `VITE_AUTH0_CLIENT_ID`, `VITE_AUTH0_AUDIENCE` in `muno/.env.example` (commit a sample) and in `muno/docs/deployment/`. Build pipeline reads these at build time.
  - **No NoETL server-side JWT validation in this round** — frontend trusts and sends the user_uid; NoETL stores it but doesn't verify the token. Document this clearly in `muno/docs/auth/auth0-setup.md` as a v1 trust assumption with a TODO for server-side validation in a follow-up round.
  - **Test fixtures**: add a vitest test that mocks `useAuth0` and verifies (a) authenticated state shows Sign out, (b) unauthenticated state shows Sign in, (c) authenticated API calls include the Bearer header.
design_brief_for_codex.container_build_with_auth0_env_vars:
The Vite build needs Auth0 values baked in at compile time. Update the Dockerfile multi-stage build to accept build-args:

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /app
ARG VITE_AUTH0_DOMAIN
ARG VITE_AUTH0_CLIENT_ID
ARG VITE_AUTH0_AUDIENCE
ARG VITE_GOOGLE_MAPS_KEY
ENV VITE_AUTH0_DOMAIN=${VITE_AUTH0_DOMAIN}
ENV VITE_AUTH0_CLIENT_ID=${VITE_AUTH0_CLIENT_ID}
ENV VITE_AUTH0_AUDIENCE=${VITE_AUTH0_AUDIENCE}
ENV VITE_GOOGLE_MAPS_KEY=${VITE_GOOGLE_MAPS_KEY}
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . ./
RUN npm run build
# ... existing nginx stage unchanged ...
```

The build script (`scripts/build_container.sh` — new) pulls all four secrets via gcloud, passes them as `--build-arg`, then `podman build` (or `docker buildx`):

```bash
VITE_AUTH0_DOMAIN=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=muno-auth0-domain --project=noetl-demo-19700101)
VITE_AUTH0_CLIENT_ID=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=muno-auth0-client-id --project=noetl-demo-19700101)
VITE_AUTH0_AUDIENCE=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=muno-auth0-audience --project=noetl-demo-19700101)
VITE_GOOGLE_MAPS_KEY=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=google-maps-widget-key --project=noetl-demo-19700101)
podman build \
  --build-arg VITE_AUTH0_DOMAIN="$VITE_AUTH0_DOMAIN" \
  --build-arg VITE_AUTH0_CLIENT_ID="$VITE_AUTH0_CLIENT_ID" \
  --build-arg VITE_AUTH0_AUDIENCE="$VITE_AUTH0_AUDIENCE" \
  --build-arg VITE_GOOGLE_MAPS_KEY="$VITE_GOOGLE_MAPS_KEY" \
  -t ghcr.io/noetl/muno:$(date -u +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S) \
  -t ghcr.io/noetl/muno:latest \
  .
```

Never echo the values. Use `set +x` if the build script has tracing enabled. Tag with a UTC timestamp + `:latest`.
design_brief_for_codex.container_registry_push: Push the built image to `ghcr.io/noetl/muno`. The repo's GitHub Actions / Codex environment should already have `GHCR_TOKEN` or equivalent set; verify in phase 1 by attempting a dry-run `podman login ghcr.io`. The image is public for the org per the noetl convention, OR private — confirm by checking how `ghcr.io/noetl/noetl` is configured. Document the chosen visibility.
design_brief_for_codex.k8s_deployment_manifests:
Add manifests at `repos/ops/ci/manifests/muno/`:

  - `namespace.yaml`: `kind: Namespace, metadata: { name: muno }` (or reuse `noetl` namespace if that's the project pattern — verify by listing existing manifests).
  - `deployment.yaml`: standard React/nginx deployment. 2 replicas. `image: ghcr.io/noetl/muno:latest` initially; pin to the timestamped tag for production stability after first successful rollout. Container port 8080. Resource requests CPU 50m / memory 64Mi; limits CPU 250m / memory 128Mi.
  - `service.yaml`: `kind: Service, type: ClusterIP, spec: { ports: [{port: 80, targetPort: 8080}], selector: app=muno }`.
  - `ingress.yaml`: ingress-nginx ingress (or whatever class the cluster uses) for `muno.mestumre.dev` with TLS termination via cert-manager. Annotation `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod` (or whichever issuer gateway.mestumre.dev uses — copy that pattern). TLS section refers to a Secret that cert-manager auto-provisions.
  - `configmap.yaml`: any runtime config muno's nginx needs. Probably empty for v1.
  - `kustomization.yaml`: kustomize entry that pulls the above + any base patches.

If the project uses Helm instead of kustomize, adapt accordingly — INSPECT `repos/ops/ci/manifests/noetl/` to determine the convention.
design_brief_for_codex.deployment_apply_and_ingress_ip_capture:
From the codex environment with `kubectl` configured for the GKE cluster:

```bash
kubectl apply -f repos/ops/ci/manifests/muno/
kubectl -n muno rollout status deployment/muno --timeout=2m  # or whatever namespace
```

Then poll for the Ingress external IP (cert-manager provisioning takes 1-3 minutes):

```bash
for i in {1..30}; do
  IP=$(kubectl -n muno get ingress muno -o jsonpath='{.status.loadBalancer.ingress[0].ip}')
  if [ -n "$IP" ]; then
    echo "INGRESS_IP=$IP"
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done
```

Output the IP. Phase 7 reports it to Kadyapam who adds the DNS record at Cloudflare per `pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.mid_round_handoff_dns`. Codex AMBERs at phase 7 awaiting DNS confirmation; resumes to phase 9 (smoke) once Kadyapam confirms.
design_brief_for_codex.live_smoke_post_dns:
After DNS is added (Kadyapam confirms):

  1. `curl -sv https://muno.mestumre.dev/` → expect HTTP 200 with `< server: nginx` header.
  2. Inspect the response HTML — should be muno's `index.html` referencing the Vite-built JS bundle.
  3. Headless Auth0 login smoke is HARD without real credentials — instead, document the manual smoke step in the result JSON for Kadyapam: 'Open https://muno.mestumre.dev/ in a browser, click Sign in, complete Auth0 flow, return to muno, verify the sidebar shows user info and the chat works.'
  4. Container deployment smoke: `kubectl -n muno get pods` → all Running, no restarts.

Check TLS via `curl -vI https://muno.mestumre.dev/ 2>&1 | grep -E 'subject|issuer'` — confirm cert is valid (Cloudflare's edge cert OR Let's Encrypt direct, depending on Cloudflare proxy setting).
design_brief_for_codex.docs_added:
- `muno/docs/deployment/muno-subdomain.md` — full GKE + Cloudflare runbook including the manifests, the kubectl apply flow, the Cloudflare DNS step, troubleshooting (cert provisioning slow, 502 from ingress, etc.).
  - `muno/docs/auth/auth0-setup.md` — frontend Auth0 integration, the SPA app registration steps Kadyapam followed, the env vars, and the v1 trust assumption (frontend-trusted user_uid; server-side JWT validation is a TODO).
  - Update `muno/README.md` to mention the live URL once deployed.
design_brief_for_codex.what_NOT_to_do:
- DO NOT add server-side JWT validation to NoETL in this round. Frontend-trusted user_uid is acceptable for v1; the round explicitly defers server validation.
  - DO NOT change Firestore security rules. They stay v1 permissive per Round 5.
  - DO NOT tighten the muno API endpoints to require auth — Guest mode remains a working fallback so anonymous users can still try the demo.
  - DO NOT commit any Auth0 secrets or token bytes. The Auth0 domain + client_id are non-secret (visible in any SPA build) and committed only via the build-arg path from gcloud.
  - DO NOT change the existing widget API key restrictions BEYOND adding the new referrer. Don't remove existing referrers (mestumre.dev, gateway.mestumre.dev, *.pages.dev).
  - DO NOT modify Cloudflare config from codex — Kadyapam owns Cloudflare access. Codex outputs DNS instructions; Kadyapam executes.

1_verify_pre_handoff.objective: Confirm Auth0 app + Maps key referrer + existing infra are ready.
spec: `gcloud secrets describe muno-auth0-domain --project=noetl-demo-19700101` → exists.
`gcloud secrets describe muno-auth0-client-id --project=noetl-demo-19700101` → exists.
`gcloud secrets describe muno-auth0-audience --project=noetl-demo-19700101` → exists.
Per-secret IAM: worker SA has `secretAccessor` on all three (the round doesn't actually need it since the values are baked into the frontend build, but operational hygiene).

`gcloud services api-keys list --project=noetl-demo-19700101 --filter='displayName:travel-agent-widget-key' --format='value(restrictions.browserKeyRestrictions.allowedReferrers)'` includes `https://muno.mestumre.dev/*`.
`kubectl config current-context` → `gke_noetl-demo-19700101_us-central1_noetl-cluster`. `kubectl -n cert-manager get certificate -A | grep mestumre` → at least one valid cert.
`kubectl get ingressclass` → records ingress class for the new ingress.
Verify ghcr.io push access (dry-run `podman login ghcr.io` or `docker login ghcr.io`).
If any check fails: AMBER + STOP.
1_verify_pre_handoff.verify: All pre-handoff items confirmed; phase 2 can proceed.

2_auth0_frontend_integration.objective: Add Auth0 to muno's React app.
spec: Per `design_brief_for_codex.auth0_frontend_integration`.
`npm install @auth0/auth0-react`.
Wire Auth0Provider, callback route, sidebar sign-in/sign-out, API client Bearer header, user_uid passthrough.
Add locale strings.
Commit `.env.example` documenting the three VITE_AUTH0_* vars (without real values).
Add vitest tests for authenticated vs unauthenticated rendering and Bearer header attachment.
`npm run type-check`, `npm run test`, `npm run smoke:widgets` all GREEN.
`npm run dev` localhost smoke: open http://localhost:5173, click Sign in, complete Auth0 flow, return to muno, confirm sidebar shows user info.

3_container_build_with_auth0_env_vars.objective: Extend Dockerfile + build script for build-time Auth0 args; build the image.
spec: Update muno's Dockerfile to accept VITE_AUTH0_DOMAIN, VITE_AUTH0_CLIENT_ID, VITE_AUTH0_AUDIENCE, VITE_GOOGLE_MAPS_KEY as build-args.
Add `muno/scripts/build_container.sh` (per design brief). chmod +x.
Run the build script; capture image ID and size.
`podman run --rm -d -p 18080:8080 ghcr.io/noetl/muno:<tag>` → curl localhost:18080 returns 200 with index.html embedding the Auth0 domain (verify via grep without echoing the full value).
Cleanup the test container.
3_container_build_with_auth0_env_vars.verify: Container builds and runs locally; image size growth bounded (target <80 MB).

4_push_image_to_ghcr.objective: Push the built image to ghcr.io/noetl/muno.
spec: `podman push ghcr.io/noetl/muno:<timestamp>` and `:latest`.
Verify via `gh api /orgs/noetl/packages/container/muno/versions --jq '.[0]' 2>&1 | head -5` or equivalent.
Capture the final image SHA digest for the deployment manifest pin.
4_push_image_to_ghcr.verify: Image is pullable via `podman pull ghcr.io/noetl/muno:latest`.

5_k8s_deployment_manifests_and_apply.objective: Add manifests at repos/ops/ci/manifests/muno/ and apply to GKE.
spec: Per `design_brief_for_codex.k8s_deployment_manifests`.
INSPECT repos/ops/ci/manifests/noetl/ first to match the project's kustomize/helm convention.
Apply via `kubectl apply -f repos/ops/ci/manifests/muno/` (or `kustomize build ... | kubectl apply -f -` if kustomize).
Wait for rollout: `kubectl -n muno rollout status deployment/muno --timeout=2m`.
Confirm pods Running: `kubectl -n muno get pods`.

6_capture_ingress_ip_and_cert_status.objective: Get the Ingress external IP + confirm cert-manager provisioning.
spec: Poll `kubectl -n muno get ingress muno -o jsonpath='{.status.loadBalancer.ingress[0].ip}'` for up to 5 minutes.
Once IP known, confirm: `kubectl -n muno get certificate -o wide` → status `Ready: True` (cert-manager provisioning can take 1-3 minutes after Ingress is up).
If cert is still provisioning at the end of phase 6, that's OK — DNS-side TLS verification happens after Cloudflare propagates the DNS record in the next phase.
Capture the Ingress IP for the result JSON.

7_output_cloudflare_dns_instructions.objective: Print clear Cloudflare DNS instructions for Kadyapam + AMBER pending DNS update.
spec: Result JSON's `dns_instructions_for_kadyapam` field contains the exact step-by-step (zone, record type, name, value, proxy setting, SSL/TLS mode confirmation).
Codex's stdout also prints these instructions.
Round status at this point: AMBER (DNS pending). Memory entry and partial result JSON capture this state.
Codex pauses here and waits for explicit user signal 'DNS added' before resuming to phase 8.

Implementation note: codex may need to break this into two task invocations if the codex flow can't pause-and-resume mid-round. If so, phases 1-7 are one Codex run; phases 8-10 are a follow-up run after Kadyapam adds DNS. Document whichever flow the codex implementation uses.
7_output_cloudflare_dns_instructions.verify: Instructions output is complete and copy-pasteable.

8_post_dns_live_smoke.objective: Verify the live URL works end-to-end after Kadyapam adds DNS.
spec: Resolve `muno.mestumre.dev` via `dig +short muno.mestumre.dev` (or `getent hosts`) — should return either the Cloudflare proxy IPs (orange-cloud mode, common case) OR the GKE Ingress IP (DNS-only mode).
`curl -sv https://muno.mestumre.dev/` → HTTP 200 with HTML body referencing muno's Vite bundle. If 521/522 (Cloudflare upstream errors), wait 30-60s for the origin cert to settle.
Confirm cert chain: `curl -vI https://muno.mestumre.dev/ 2>&1 | grep -E 'subject|issuer'` → if Cloudflare proxy: Cloudflare-issued cert; if DNS-only: cert-manager Let's Encrypt cert.

Document the manual browser smoke step for Kadyapam: 'Open https://muno.mestumre.dev/ in a browser → click Sign in → complete Auth0 → return to muno → verify sidebar shows user info → start a trip planning conversation → confirm Firestore writes a chat_threads document under users/{your_auth0_sub}/.'
Re-confirm the Firestore writes work: `firestore_replay.sh thread-list --limit 3` → find Kadyapam's signed-in thread (path includes user_uid).
8_post_dns_live_smoke.verify: URL serves muno; browser-side login flow described to user.

9_docs_and_close_out.objective: Author runbooks, memory, result JSON, PRs.
spec: Author `muno/docs/deployment/muno-subdomain.md` — full GKE + Cloudflare + Auth0 runbook.
Author `muno/docs/auth/auth0-setup.md` — Auth0 SPA registration steps + frontend integration notes + the v1 trust-assumption with the server-side-validation TODO.
Update `muno/README.md` with the live URL.
Update `muno/docs/deployment/kind-setup.md` to note the new build-arg surface area (the dev workflow now needs the four VITE_* vars).

Result file: `bridge/outbox/20260513-040000-muno-auth0-deploy.result.json`. Required: id, status, summary, muno_pr_url, muno_merge_sha, ops_pr_url, ops_merge_sha, ai_meta_submodule_pointer_bump_shas, image_sha, ingress_ip, dns_added_at, live_url_curl_status, cert_issuer, auth0_smoke_described (bool), cleanup, verdict.
Append a paragraph to `ai-meta/bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md`.
Memory entry at `ai-meta/memory/inbox/2026/05/<timestamp>-muno-auth0-deploy-green.md` (or AMBER if anything didn't land cleanly).
Update `ai-meta/sync/issues/2026-05-12-trip-planner-app-scoping.md` adding a 'Round 8 (post-tutorial): muno deployed + Auth0' status note.
Open muno PR: `feat(auth+deploy): Auth0 SPA integration + muno.mestumre.dev deployment manifests`.

Open repos/ops PR (if k8s manifests landed there): `feat(deploy): muno trip-planner ingress + manifests`. Some projects keep deploy manifests in muno itself rather than ops — INSPECT and follow the project convention.
ai-meta side: bump `repos/muno` (and `repos/ops` if applicable) submodule pointers locally. Do NOT push ai-meta.
Pin the deployment image to the timestamped tag (not :latest) in the manifest after the first successful rollout — update the manifest in a follow-up commit to repos/ops main.
9_docs_and_close_out.verify: All artefacts written; PRs merged; ai-meta pointers bumped locally.

architectural_rules: Auth0 frontend-only in v1. NO server-side JWT validation in this round.
Guest mode preserved as fallback (anonymous users can still demo).
Firestore security rules unchanged from v1 permissive.
Auth0 domain + client_id baked into the build via build-args, NOT served at runtime. Standard SPA pattern.
Container image goes to ghcr.io/noetl/muno (mirror the noetl/noetl image convention).
Deployment manifests live in repos/ops (or muno — verify convention). Either way, single PR per repo.
Cloudflare DNS update is Kadyapam-side. Codex outputs instructions and AMBERs pending confirmation.
Cloudflare proxy mode RECOMMENDED (orange cloud) — gets us Cloudflare's TLS termination + WAF.

If proxy mode is on and Cloudflare's edge cert covers `muno.mestumre.dev` via the existing zone wildcard, the cert-manager TLS at the origin still works because Cloudflare → origin uses 'Full (Strict)' validation against the cert-manager cert.
Maps key gains `https://muno.mestumre.dev/*` referrer; other referrers unchanged.
Never commit Auth0 secrets or any token bytes. Auth0 domain + client_id are non-secret but flow via gcloud secrets for ops cleanliness.
muno PR + ops PR via standard flow.
ai-meta pointer bumps local-only; Kadyapam pushes ai-meta.
result_file_instructions: Write `bridge/outbox/20260513-040000-muno-auth0-deploy.result.json`. Required: id, status (GREEN once live smoke passes; AMBER pending DNS is a valid intermediate state), summary, muno_pr_url, muno_merge_sha, ops_pr_url (if applicable), ops_merge_sha (if applicable), ai_meta_submodule_pointer_bump_shas, image_uri (with sha digest), ingress_ip, dns_instructions_for_kadyapam (full text), dns_added_at_iso, live_url_curl_status, cert_issuer, browser_auth0_smoke_described, manual_smoke_checklist_for_kadyapam, cleanup, verdict.
references: muno repo at fdd7dc9 / 96759b8 / current main
